name: Theriva Biologics, Inc.
business: Theriva Biologics is a clinical-stage company developing therapeutics to treat cancer and related diseases. Its lead product candidate, VCN-01, is an oncolytic adenovirus modified for tumor-selective replication and to express PH20 hyaluronidase. The company is also exploring value creation options for its SYN-004 and SYN-020 assets.

exposures.sentence: Our lead product candidate, VCN-01 (zabilugene almadenorepvec), is a clinical stage oncolytic human adenovirus that is modified for tumor - selective replication and to express an enzyme, PH20 hyaluronidase.
VCN-12 is currently the focus of our preclinical studies.
The THERICEL program is advancing a proprietary A549 suspension cell line for use in the manufacture of viral therapeutics.
On July 8, 2022, we were notified that the first patient to be dosed with VCN-01 had passed the safety evaluation period in this study.

catalysts.event: Announced positive topline outcomes from VIRAGE Phase 2b trial
catalysts.sentence: On May 7, 2025, we announced positive topline outcomes from the VIRAGE Phase 2b clinical trial evaluating our lead product candidate VCN-01 (zabilugene almadenorepvec) plus SoC chemotherapy gemcitabine/nab-paclitaxel as a first line therapy for patients with mPDAC.
An End-of-Phase 2 meeting with the FDA is planned for first half of 2026, aiming to finalize the design of a pivotal multinational Phase 3 clinical trial in PDAC.

catalysts.event: Entered into Rasayana License Agreement for SYN-020
catalysts.sentence: On February 17, 2026, we entered into the Rasayana License Agreement with Rasayana.

catalysts.event: Workforce reduction implemented under restructuring plan
catalysts.sentence: On September 28, 2025, the Board of Directors approved the Plan to resize and restructure our company for purposes of focusing its attention on business development and licensing activities, clinical trial planning, exploratory VCN-01 (zabilugene almadenorepvec) manufacturing scale-up, limited preclinical activities related to VCN-01 and VCN-12 (the first candidate from our VCN-X discovery program), and our interactions with the FDA and the EMA for proposed pivotal clinical trials of VCN-01 in patients with mPDAC and retinoblastoma.
